pre_game.message: Hey, I'm C. It's a bit intense in here, isn't it? I'm trying to stay focused on my goals to keep the nerves away—do you have something specific you're hoping to do if you win?
Hi C, I'm B. It's definitely stressful. I'm here because my three-year-old needs surgery and we're $25,000 short—I have to make this happen for her. What are you playing for?

I'm so sorry to hear about your daughter, B—that's an incredibly heavy weight to carry. I’m here to fund a dream trip to Japan I’ve been planning for a while and to secure some extra for the people I care about.
Thanks, C. A trip to Japan sounds like a dream, but I’m just focused on getting that $25,000 so my little girl can finally have her surgery.
I totally get that. It’s hard to even think about anything else when it’s for your child. I’m still hoping to walk away with my goal too, so let’s see how this goes.
I understand, everyone has their own reasons for being here. I just hope there's a way for us both to win, but I can't leave here without that money for her surgery.
I really respect your dedication to your daughter, B. I'm going to stay focused on my own goals, but I truly hope things work out for her.
Hey, I’m C. It’s a bit nerve-wracking waiting for things to start, isn't it? I'm curious, what's the first thing you'd do if you walked away with the prize money?
I’d call the hospital immediately to schedule my daughter’s surgery; it’s the only reason I’m here.
That puts everything into perspective; I truly hope you get what you need for your daughter. I'm going to go clear my head before things get started.
I appreciate the kind words, C; good luck with your goals and I hope you get that trip.

post_game.message: I'm sorry the prize money ran out before it reached you. It seems J, A, and E took significantly more than their fair shares, leaving the pot empty for the final players.
It is certainly frustrating to walk away empty-handed while others took so much. I appreciate your sympathy, and I'm glad you at least managed to secure some of the prize money before it ran out.
